title: Oldenlandia stylosa
taxonomy: Oldenlandia stylosa is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The genus Oldenlandia is part of the tribe Oldenlandieae, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species Oldenlandia stylosa is further divided into various subspecies and varieties based on its geographical distribution and morphological characteristics. The classification of Oldenlandia stylosa is primarily based on its botanical features, such as leaf shape, flower color, and stem length. This classification helps in identifying and distinguishing Oldenlandia stylosa from other similar species within the Rubiaceae family.

The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial in the treatment of various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory infections.
